title: Crawl Graph, Frontier State Machine, Scheduling Identity, and Invariants · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for crawl graph, frontier state machine, scheduling identity, and invariants and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deterministic frontier transition model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, failed, retryable, terminal, and canceled transitions.
Atomically reserve admitted URL identity when scheduling so concurrent discovery cannot duplicate effects.
Prioritize owner commitments, depth, page classes, freshness, origin fairness, and retries with stable tie rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz State Invariants
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 2797 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove legal frontier states, reservation timing, stable priorities, graph evidence, and invariants; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Check unique active ownership, bounded work, legal transitions, terminal immutability, count reconciliation, and recoverability.
